POINTS:
- [elytron/src/main/java/org/wildfly/extension/elytron/ClassLoadingAttributeDefinitions.java:25] The idea has always been to place this sort of conversions/adjustments as close as possible to the external inputs, hence on the attribute definitions via `ModuleIdentifierUtil.MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR`, however, for this [JaasCustomSecurityRealmWrapper.java](https://github.com/wildfly/wildfly-core/blob/bb0806bacc370a49141f0c9cd9be4fb12066a0bb/elytron/src/main/java/org/wildfly/extension/elytron/JaasCustomSecurityRealmWrapper.java) class, which is indeed deprecated, I'm not sure what the external input is for this `configuration` object
v8_review:
SUMMARY:
This PR applies `ModuleIdentifierUtil.MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR` to module-name attributes across the codebase and removes now-redundant `parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier` calls in handlers. The core pattern is correct — the corrector canonicalizes values at write time (stripping `:main` suffix, preserving non-main slots), so handlers can use the raw value directly. However, some changes add the corrector to read-only attributes where it has no effect.

POINTS:
- [logging/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/logging/deployments/resources/HandlerResourceDefinition.java:33-35] The `MODULE` attribute is registered as **read-only** via `registerReadOnlyAttribute()` (line 84). Parameter correctors in the WildFly controller framework only execute during write operations (add/write-attribute), never during read. Adding `.setCorrector(MODULE_NAME_CORRECTOR)` here has **no effect**. Recommend reverting this change.
- [logging/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/logging/deployments/resources/LoggingDeploymentResources.java:121-123] Same issue — `MODULE` in `PropertiesResourceDefinition` is registered as read-only (line 144 via `registerReadOnlyAttribute()`). The corrector will never be invoked. Recommend reverting this change.
- [logging/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/logging/deployments/resources/HandlerResourceDefinition.java:7] and [logging/src/main/java/org/wildfly/extension/security/manager/DeploymentPermissionsResourceDefinition.java:21] The `import org.jboss.as.controller.ModuleIdentifierUtil;` added for the two logging deployment files is effectively dead code since the corrector is never used on read-only attributes.
- [elytron/src/main/java/org/wildfly/extension/elytron/DirContextDefinition.java:127-130] The `MODULE` attribute here has `.setAllowExpression(true)`. The corrector will run on literal values, which is correct, but be aware that if a user supplies an expression (e.g., `${some.module.name}`), the corrector will see the unresolved expression string — not the resolved module name. This is existing framework behavior and not a bug, just worth noting.
- [elytron/src/main/java/org/wildfly/extension/elytron/ClassLoadingAttributeDefinitions.java:44-45] Removing `ModuleIdentifierUtil.parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier(module)` and using `module` directly is correct because the corrector already canonicalized the value when the attribute was written. Same pattern correctly applied in DirContextDefinition.java:161, DiscoveryOptionAddHandler.java:75-76, and ModuleLoadingResourceDefinition.java:208.
- [controller/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/controller/extension/ExtensionResourceDefinition.java:35-36] Adding the corrector to the **deprecated** `module` parameter of `ADD_OP` is harmless and provides consistency, though deprecated parameters are no longer actively used. No issue here.
- [logging/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/logging/CommonAttributes.java:113-125] Changing `"module"`, `"name"`, `"properties"` to `ModelDescriptionConstants.MODULE`, `.NAME`, `.PROPERTIES` is a benign style improvement — the constant values are identical strings. No functional change.
